title: Asirgarh Fort
tagline: Asirgarh Fort — heritage city of Madhya Pradesh

overview.short: Asirgarh Fort is an Indian fortress (qila) situated in the Satpura Range about 20 kilometres (12 mi) north of the city of Burhanpur, in the Indian state of Madhya Pradesh.
overview.description: Asirgarh Fort is an Indian fortress (qila) situated in the Satpura Range about 20 kilometres (12 mi) north of the city of Burhanpur, in the Indian state of Madhya Pradesh. The fort is said to date to the early 15th century and commands a pass through the Satpuras connecting the valleys of the Narmada and Tapti rivers, one of the most important routes from northern India to the Deccan, it was known as the "key to the Deccan".

topPlaces.name: Keoti Fort
topPlaces.description: Keoti Fort (also Keonti Fort) is an Indian fortress (qila) situated on the Rewa Plateau about 53 kilometres (33 mi) north-east of the city of Rewa, in Rewa district in the Indian state of Madhya Pradesh.

items.note: Keoti Fort (also Keonti Fort) is an Indian fortress (qila) situated on the Rewa Plateau about 53 kilometres (33 mi) north-east of the city of Rewa, in Rewa district in the Indian state of Madhya Pradesh.

faq.q: What is the best time to visit Asirgarh Fort?
faq.a: The best time to visit Asirgarh Fort is Oct – Mar. Summers are 26–40°C and winters are 12–28°C.

faq.q: How do I reach Asirgarh Fort?
faq.a: The nearest airport is Jalgaon Airport (89 km) and the nearest railway station is Itarsi Junction (197 km). Roads are generally good; check seasonal/monsoon conditions before high-altitude or remote stretches.

faq.q: How many days are enough for Asirgarh Fort?
faq.a: 2 days are usually enough to cover the top attractions of Asirgarh Fort at a relaxed pace.

faq.q: How much does a hotel cost in Asirgarh Fort?
faq.a: Stays in Asirgarh Fort start from ₹900 per night, with options across 4 price categories.

faq.q: What are the top places to visit in Asirgarh Fort?
faq.a: The top attractions are Keoti Fort.
